summary: Beijing’s grip over the rare earths industry will last years, heavily influencing US-China ties.

title_cn: 中国铝业同意斥资10亿美元建设几内亚氧化铝厂

title_cn: 特朗普想要乌克兰的无人机技术
summary: The US is testing some of the war-torn country’s military hardware with the view to closing a deal.

title: China Launches Major Crackdown on Illegal Securities Trading
title_cn: 中国严厉打击非法证券交易
summary: China has launched an unprecedented campaign against illegal cross-border trading, threatening severe penalties against popular brokers and ordering existing non-compliant accounts to be liquidated within two years.

title: Rupee Plunge Sees India Turn to 2013 Taper Tantrum Playbook
title_cn: 卢比暴跌导致印度转向 2013 年缩减恐慌策略
summary: India’s central bank may need to draw on its 2013 taper tantrum playbook and earlier balance-of-payments crises to mount an effective defense of the beleaguered rupee.

title: China Restricts Chemical Exports to US in Fentanyl Crackdown
title_cn: 中国限制向美国出口化学品以打击芬太尼
summary: China imposed new export controls on some key chemical ingredients shipped to the US, Mexico and Canada, in a further sign of cooperation with Washington on curbing drug trafficking.

title: Buyout Bankers Go Big on Debt Pre-Sales to Thwart Fickle Markets
title_cn: 收购银行家大肆进行债务预售以阻止变化无常的市场
summary: Timing can make or break a leveraged debt sale. Get it right, and banks secure cheap financing for their client and maximize fees. Get it wrong, and they can end up with their profits — and reputation — on the line.

title: US Stocks Eye Best Weekly Run of Gains Since 2023: Markets Wrap
title_cn: 美股有望创 2023 年以来最佳单周涨幅：市场总结
summary: The S&P 500 is heading for its longest streak of weekly wins since 2023 as hopes that the US and Iran are moving closer to a peace deal and unrelenting enthusiasm for artificial intelligence keep on driving equities higher.

title: ECB Chief Says Inflation Expectations Remain On Target
title_cn: 欧洲央行行长表示通胀预期仍处于目标范围内
summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde stressed that long-term inflation expectations are still broadly in line with the 2% target, despite deepening fallout from the Iran war.

title: UBS Hits Back Over Nazi-Accounts Probe Amid Senate Pressure
title_cn: 瑞银在参议院压力下反击纳粹账户调查
summary: UBS Group AG accused the US lawyer overseeing a six-year-old inquiry into Credit Suisse’s handling of Nazi-linked accounts of bias and exceeding his mandate, deepening a standoff between the Swiss bank and its critics in Washington.

title: Oil Rises After Three-Day Drop With Iran-US Talks in Focus
title_cn: 油价连续三天下跌后上涨，伊朗与美国谈判成为焦点
summary: Oil rose, snapping a three-day decline, as Iran’s comments on uranium and the Strait of Hormuz pared earlier optimism over progress in peace negotiations with the US.

title: Superbonus Legacy Is Making Italy’s Debt Stand Out, BE Says
title_cn: BE表示，超级奖金遗产正在使意大利的债务脱颖而出
summary: Italy’s increasing debt burden risks mounting scrutiny from investors despite the efforts of Giorgia Meloni’s government to bring it under control, according to Bloomberg Economics.

title: Samsung Chip Workers Face Colleagues’ Resentment Over Bonus Deal
title_cn: 三星芯片工人面临同事对奖金交易的不满
summary: Samsung Electronics Co. staved off a potentially catastrophic strike this week, reaching a tentative deal with leaders of its largest union at the last minute to share more profits from the AI boom. But inside the $1.1 trillion technology behemoth, not everyone is happy.

title: Euro-Zone Wage Growth Slowed Before War Drove Inflation Up
title_cn: 战前欧元区工资增长放缓导致通胀上升
summary: A key measure of euro-area pay growth slowed, providing some relief to European Central Bank officials worrying about emerging inflation risks from the Iran war.

title: China Scrutinizes Firms, Funds After AI-Fueled Stock Moves
title_cn: 人工智能推动股票走势后，中国对公司和基金进行审查
summary: China’s stock exchanges are scrutinizing recent stock rallies that have been fueled by artificial intelligence optimism, asking some listed companies and funds to give more details about their approach to the technology, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Worst-Case Scenario Looks More Likely for European Stocks
title_cn: 欧洲股市更有可能出现最坏的情况
summary: Unless the Strait of Hormuz reopens soon, the case for European stocks will degrade materially, according to strategists surveyed by Bloomberg.

title: South Korea to Trim Long-Term Bond Sales as Part of June Cut
title_cn: 作为六月减产的一部分，韩国将削减长期债券销售
summary: South Korea will mainly cut issuance of longer-dated tenor notes for its planned reduction in June bond sales, according to a top finance ministry official.

title: Tycoon Puts Billions Into Electric Cars as Stock Booms 1000%
title_cn: 大亨向电动汽车投入数十亿美元，股价暴涨 1000%
summary: Vietnam’s richest person spent billions of dollars of his personal fortune on his emerging electric automaker and other ventures last year as the shares of his corporate group soared.

title: South Korea Says Won Moves Excessive, Warns of Market Actions
title_cn: 韩国称韩元波动过度，警告市场行为
summary: South Korea stepped up verbal intervention to counter the won’s decline against the dollar, warning that authorities would “take decisive actions if necessary” to stabilize the currency.

title: China to Penalize Tiger, Futu in Cross-Border Flow Crackdown
title_cn: 中国将在打击跨境流动中处罚泰格、富途
summary: China’s securities regulator plans to penalize Futu Holdings Ltd., UP Fintech Holding Ltd.’s Tiger Brokers and Longbridge Securities Ltd. for operating on the mainland without a license, marking a sharp escalation in a yearslong campaign against illicit cross-border brokerages.

title: The Great Hotel Room Blanding
title_cn: 伟大的酒店房间布兰丁
summary: It's getting harder and harder to tell the difference between a $300-a-night room and one that costs many more times that. Bloomberg's Brandon Presser explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: London Traders Are Stuck With an Ultra-Long Bond Bet Gone Bad
title_cn: 伦敦交易员因超长期债券押注失败而陷入困境
summary: It was supposed to be a relatively quick and lucrative bet. Buy a long-dated UK government bond at an historic discount to par, wait for the price to pop and then reap a tax-free windfall.

title: Richemont Sales Climb on Resilient Demand for Jewelry
title_cn: 珠宝需求强劲，历峰集团销售额攀升
summary: Richemont’s full-year sales rose as shoppers splurged on its pricey Cartier bracelets and rings, helping the Swiss group weather a luxury market slowdown better than most rivals.

title: German Business Outlook Improves for First Time Since War
title_cn: 德国商业前景自战后首次改善
summary: Germany’s business outlook improved for the first time since fighting broke out in the Middle East, demonstrating some resilience to the war for Europe’s biggest economy.

title: Turkey Stocks Start Bouncing Back After Court Blow to Opposition
title_cn: 法院打击反对派后土耳其股市开始反弹
summary: The pressure on Turkish stocks eased following a selloff sparked by a Turkish court decision on Thursday to remove the leadership of the country’s main opposition party.

title: Mol Shares Drop After Petrochemicals Plant Explosion in Hungary
title_cn: 匈牙利石化厂爆炸后摩尔股价下跌
summary: Shares in Mol Nyrt., Hungary’s biggest energy group, dropped after an explosion at its petrochemicals plant in eastern Hungary killed one person and seriously injured several others.

title: 'The Assassin' Fahmi Quadir on How to Survive as a Short-Seller | Odd Lots
title_cn: “刺客”法赫米·夸迪尔 (Fahmi Quadir) 谈如何作为卖空者生存零股
summary: A short-seller is a gumshoe who roots out a particular story about a specific company and brings it to light. In this way, a short-seller is similar to an investigative journalist. Fahmi Quadir, the founder and CIO of Safkhet Capital, is known as "the Assassin." Her notoriety proceeds her: Among her most well-known bets include short-selling Wirecard AG and Valeant. In today's conversation with Qaudir — recorded at our live show in London at Wilton's Music Hall —  she dishes on what life is like for a short-seller, especially one as famous as her. In her mind, short-selling is only getting harder; it's a corner of finance that is facing an existential crisis and one whose value is questioned in what she calls a "golden age of fraud." She also tells us, that for the first time ever, she is going long with a focus on Korea that has nothing to do with the AI boom. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Philippines’ BSP Flags Bold Rate Move to Stay Ahead of Inflation
title_cn: 菲律宾央行表示将采取大胆的利率举措以领先于通胀
summary: Philippine central bank Governor Eli Remolona said monetary authorities will have to react aggressively to avoid falling behind the curve in taming price pressures spurred by the Iran war.

title: Total Explores Selling Stakes in European Green Assets
title_cn: 道达尔探索出售欧洲绿色资产股份
summary: TotalEnergies SE is exploring selling a 50% stake in some of its European renewables assets, as the French energy giant seeks a partner for its green investments, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Julius Baer Shares Slump as Net New Money Figure Disappoints
title_cn: 净新资金数据令人失望，瑞士宝盛股价暴跌
summary: Julius Baer Group Ltd. saw client inflows slow for the first four months of the year, with the bank’s continued de-risking efforts weighing on the Swiss wealth manager’s growth aspirations.

title: Short-Seller Dubbed ‘The Assassin’ Is Going Long Korea Stocks
title_cn: 被称为“刺客”的卖空者正在做多韩国股市
summary: One of the most prominent names among a shrinking group of short-sellers who make their money by betting against stocks has a new strategy: going long the world’s best-performing market.

title: China’s AI-Made Video Is Changing the Entertainment Landscape
title_cn: 中国人工智能视频正在改变娱乐格局
summary: Such services pose an existential threat to traditional entertainment.

title: Zara Owner Inditex’s CEO Bets on Diversification, AI for Growth
title_cn: Zara 所有者 Inditex 首席执行官押注多元化和人工智能促进增长
summary: Zara owner Inditex SA is banking on diversification across brands and countries as well as artificial intelligence to spur growth at the world’s largest listed retailer, Chief Executive Officer Óscar García Maceiras said.

title: BlackRock's Li Sees Earnings Momentum Driven by AI
title_cn: 贝莱德李嘉诚认为人工智能将推动盈利增长
summary: BlackRock Chief Investment Strategist Wei Li says artificial intelligence is driving a lot of the major earnings upgrades in equity markets. "We can look at AI lifting the whole market, but identifying where the bottlenecks are is a good active play," Li tells Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Salesforce Touts AI Promise Over Reality in SaaSpocalypse Fight
title_cn: Salesforce 在 SaaS 末日之战中宣扬人工智能的承诺而非现实
summary: Patients at the University of Chicago Medicine featured in a promotional video seamlessly refill prescriptions, book appointments and even get parking tips with the help of Agentforce, Salesforce Inc.’s flagship AI tool.

title: Randstad CEO on AI & Future of Work
title_cn: 任仕达首席执行官谈人工智能和未来工作
summary:
Sander van ’t Noordende, CEO of recruitment giant Randstad, says their base case is that 7-8% of jobs will be replaced by artificial intelligence over the next 5 to 10 years. However, Van ‘t Noordende says he does not see the "doomsday scenario" that some predict.

In an interview on "The Pulse with Francine Lacqua" on May 21, van ‘t Noordende said AI will be a "wave" over the next decade, but also presents many opportunities, enabling many workers to be more productive, and requires massive infrastructure. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Cartoon ‘Critterz’ Misses Cannes Debut After OpenAI Shut Sora
title_cn: OpenAI 关闭 Sora 后，AI 卡通片《Critterz》错过了戛纳首映
summary: Critterz, a feature-length cartoon intended to showcase how OpenAI’s video-generation capabilities could revolutionize filmmaking, has missed its planned Cannes Film Festival debut after the artificial intelligence company shut down its Sora tool, forcing its creators to look for a new AI partner.

title: StanChart CEO Apologizes for ‘Lower-Value Human’ Comments
title_cn: 渣打银行首席执行官为“低价值人类”言论道歉
summary: Standard Chartered Plc Chief Executive Officer Bill Winters apologized for his comments earlier this week about how artificial intelligence will affect “lower-value human capital,” after such remarks spurred public backlash.

title: EU-Anthropic Talks Over Mythos Concerns Are Stalled, Spain Says
title_cn: 西班牙表示，欧盟与人类关于神话问题的谈判陷入僵局
summary: The European Union has made little progress in talks with Anthropic PBC to get its banks and companies tested for digital vulnerabilities that the new Mythos AI model uncovers, Spain’s economy minister said.

title: Polymarket Is Said to Seek Japan Prediction Market Approval
title_cn: 据称 Polymarket 正在寻求日本预测市场的批准
summary: Polymarket has appointed a representative in Japan and is preparing to lobby for the authorization of prediction markets in the country, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Chinese Brands Snap Up Record 15% of Europe’s Electric-Car Sales
title_cn: 中国品牌抢占欧洲电动汽车销量 15% 创历史新高
summary: Chinese automakers accounted for more than 15% of electric-vehicle sales in Europe for the first time last month as consumer appetite for models from the country shows no sign of slowing.

title: Kawasaki Heavy Shares Rally on AI Tie-Up Plan With Nvidia
title_cn: 川崎重工因与 Nvidia 的人工智能合作计划而股价上涨
summary: Kawasaki Heavy Industries Ltd. shares rallied as much as 12%, the most since February, on the company’s plan to collaborate with US tech company Nvidia Corp. and others on physical AI robot technology.

title: Vuchot on Why Luxury Travel Is Resilient in Asia
title_cn: Vuchot 谈为何豪华旅游在亚洲具有弹性
summary:
Luxury hospitality is proving resilient despite geopolitical risk, fuel shortages and market volatility. This segment examines how travel demand is shifting within Asia, with domestic and regional travel supporting occupancy and pricing.
Benjamin Vuchot, CEO of The Hongkong and Shanghai Hotels, speaks with Haslinda Amin from the JPMorgan Global China Summit in Shanghai to discuss demand trends, operational challenges and the group’s strategic priorities. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Insilico on Applying AI to Transform Drug Discovery
title_cn: Insilico 致力于应用人工智能变革药物发现
summary: Insilico Medicine Founder & CEO Alex Zhavoronkov discusses how his company aims to apply artificial intelligence to revolutionize drug discovery and longevity research. He speaks with Haslinda Amin from the sidelines of 'JPMorgan Global China Summit' in Shanghai. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Capex Drives Memory Demand and Asia Tech Outlook
title_cn: 人工智能资本支出推动内存需求和亚洲科技前景
summary: Rising investment in artificial intelligence is reshaping demand for semiconductors, with memory, CPUs and GPUs all seeing sustained growth. This segment examines how AI-driven capex is supporting companies across Asia’s tech supply chain. Jonathan Curtis, Portfolio Manager at Franklin Templeton, speaks with Haslinda Amin from the JPMorgan Global China Summit in Shanghai to share his outlook on AI, memory markets and global equities. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lenovo Surges to 26-Year High After Strong AI Growth | The China Show 5/22/2026
title_cn: 人工智能强劲增长后，联想股价飙升至 26 年来新高 |中国展 2026 年 5 月 22 日
summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and David Ingles give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Investors Look Beyond TSMC as AI Boom Spreads to New Winners
title_cn: 随着人工智能热潮蔓延至新的赢家，投资者将目光投向台积电之外
summary: After several years flying high as Asia’s best Nvidia Corp. proxy, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. is increasingly vying with other artificial intelligence stocks for investor attention.

title: Lenovo Shares Near All-Time High After Strong AI Growth
title_cn: 人工智能强劲增长后，联想股价接近历史新高
summary: Lenovo Group Ltd. shares were on track to close at their highest on Friday after the company reported strong growth in AI-related earnings that offset difficulties from rising component prices.

title: StanChart Fields Regulator Queries After CEO’s AI Remarks
title_cn: 渣打银行首席执行官发表人工智能言论后引发监管机构质疑
summary: Regulators in Hong Kong and Singapore are said to have sought clarity from Standard Chartered following CEO Bill Winters' "lower-value human capital" comments. Bloomberg's Avril Hong reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Nintendo Seeks to Top Conservative Switch Forecast by About 20%
title_cn: 任天堂寻求比保守的 Switch 预测高出约 20%
summary: Nintendo Co. has asked partners and suppliers to assemble about 20 million Switch 2 consoles in the year through March, roughly 20% more than the public sales outlook it issued earlier this month, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Nidec CEO Kishida Hints at More M&A Deals Once Scandals Abate
title_cn: 日本电产首席执行官岸田暗示一旦丑闻消退将进行更多并购交易
summary: Nidec Corp., once one of Japan’s most acquisitive companies, will return to dealmaking once it recovers from scandals over accounting, succession and quality control, Chief Executive Officer Mitsuya Kishida said.

title: Samsung Foundry Partner SemiFive CEO on Earnings
title_cn: 三星代工合作伙伴 SemiFive 首席执行官谈收益
summary:
Brandon Cho, Co-founder and CEO of custom AI semiconductor designer, SemiFive, discusses the company's 137-percent year‑on‑year surge in revenue in its first earnings release since listing on the Kosdaq in December.
The Seoul‑based company says AI demand has driven production bookings up to 74-percent in the first quarter alone.
He speaks exclusively with Haidi Stroud Watts on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Nidec CEO: Data Center Demand Offsets Scandal Impact
title_cn: 日本电产首席执行官：数据中心需求抵消了丑闻的影响
summary: Mitsuya Kishida, CEO of Nidec, says the company's recent financial performance is being bolstered by its data-center business, despite quality control and accounting problems at the supplier of electric motors. He says 'regaining trust' with customers is the firm's first priority. He speaks with Shery Ahn on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Netflix, Disney to Face Higher Costs Under New Canada Streaming Rules
title_cn: 根据加拿大新的流媒体规则，Netflix、迪士尼将面临更高的成本
summary: Canada will require streaming giants including Netflix Inc. to spend 15% of their annual Canadian revenue on local content, moving ahead with rules that have been identified as a trade issue by the US.

title: Waymo Takes Multi-City Pause on Floods, Halts Freeway Access
title_cn: Waymo 因洪水而在多个城市暂停运营，并停止高速公路通行
summary: Waymo has temporarily halted service in five cities over concerns that its robotaxis may attempt to drive on flooded roads, the latest setback over an issue that recently led the company to recall thousands of vehicles.

title: Spacex’s IPO & the Future of the AI Build-Out | The Close 5/21/2026
title_cn: SpaceX 的 IPO 和人工智能建设的未来 |收盘价 2026 年 5 月 21 日
summary: Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are Gerber Kawasaki Investment Management’s Ross Gerber, 1789 Capital Partner Paul Abrahimzadeh, Space Capital Founder & CEO Chad Anderson, Research Affiliates Director of Research Campbell Harvey, Man Group’s Matt Rowe, Coresight Research Founder & CEO Deborah Weinswig, RXO CEO Drew Wilkerson, Advanced Machine Intelligence Executive Chair Yann LeCun & Bioptimus Co-Founder & CEO Jean-Philippe Vert, & New York City Comptroller Mark Levine. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The Next Phase of Artificial Intelligence
title_cn: 人工智能的下一阶段
summary: Yann LeCun & JP Vert discuss artificial intelligence and LLMs, and how they can translate into the physical world in the future. The two talk about the new techniques and infrastructures that will need to be built, as well as where some of the physical AI components can be built. They speak with Roman Bostick on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Workday Rallies After Results Quiet Fears of AI Disruption
title_cn: 业绩公布后工作日反弹，平息了对人工智能颠覆的担忧
summary: Workday Inc., a maker of workplace management software, posted better-than-expected results in the first quarter, helping quell concerns that the company will be disrupted by artificial intelligence.

title: Cursor Hits $3 Billion Annual Sales Rate Ahead of SpaceX Deal
title_cn: Cursor 在 SpaceX 交易之前年销售额达到 30 亿美元
summary: Cursor’s annualized revenue hit $3 billion in late April, according to a person familiar with the matter, indicating growing demand for the company’s AI coding software ahead of an expected deal to be acquired by Elon Musk’s SpaceX. The artificial intelligence startup’s annualized revenue, which projects sales for a 12-month-period based on recent performance, topped $2 billion in February, Bloomberg previously reported. Cursor now has more than 3,000 customers paying at least $100,000 each for

title: Kosovo Approves Cash Handouts 16 Days Ahead of Snap Elections
title_cn: 科索沃在提前选举前 16 天批准现金发放
summary: Kosovo’s government approved cash handouts and higher maternity benefits 16 days before snap elections in which Prime Minister Albin Kurti is seeking re-election after months of political deadlock.

title: Trump Should Revive This Hall Of Fame
title_cn: 特朗普应该重振这座名人堂
summary: Trump wants to build the National Garden of Heroes, but he should instead look to the first Hall of Fame hidden on the beautiful campus of Bronx Community College, says Bloomberg Opinion columnist Frank Barry. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bolivia’s New President Is In Trouble After Just Six Months
title_cn: 玻利维亚新总统上任仅六个月就陷入麻烦
summary: Rodrigo Paz was elected with broad support to tackle an economic crisis. Now many just want him to go.

title: EU Reviewing Italy’s Call to Ease Fiscal Rules as Costs Rise
title_cn: 欧盟正在审查意大利因成本上升而放松财政规则的呼吁
summary: The European Union is considering Italy’s plea to get more leeway on the bloc’s fiscal rules as the Iran war raises costs for households and businesses.

title: Why Prabowo’s Commodity Export Controls Are Spooking Indonesian Markets
title_cn: 为什么普拉博沃的商品出口管制令印尼市场感到不安
summary: Indonesia’s President Prabowo Subianto has been undertaking an ambitious agenda to try to remake Southeast Asia’s biggest economy. The centerpiece is an effort to lift annual growth to 8%, up from an average of roughly 5% over the past decade.

title: Russia Is ‘Guiding’ Drones Into NATO Airspace, Estonia Says
title_cn: 爱沙尼亚称俄罗斯正在“引导”无人机进入北约领空
summary: Estonian Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna accused Russia of “guiding” stray Ukrainian military drones toward NATO countries after a series of airspace incursions caused disruption in the Baltic states this week.

title: US-Iran Talks Progress Undermined By Hormuz, Uranium Divide
title_cn: 美伊谈判进展因霍尔木兹海峡和铀鸿沟而受到破坏
summary: An attack on a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates has raised fears about the scope of Iran’s retaliation to a potential US resumption of strikes, with experts highlighting the greater role Tehran-backed militias in Iraq are playing in the war. Meanwhile, the US and Iran are seeking to turn a fragile ceasefire into a more permanent end to fighting, but have yet to reach an agreement. Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar reports from Dubai. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ukraine Says It Targeted Russia’s Yaroslavl Oil Refinery Again
title_cn: 乌克兰称再次针对俄罗斯雅罗斯拉夫尔炼油厂
summary: Ukraine targeted the Yaroslavl oil refinery in central Russia overnight to inflict further damage on its foe’s energy infrastructure, according to President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

title: Can Cuba’s Regime Survive Rising US Pressure?
title_cn: 古巴政权能否承受美国不断加大的压力？
summary: Ever since US Special Forces captured Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro on Jan. 3 and launched an attack on Iran, Washington has been warning that Cuba will be next. Under a US-imposed economic blockade, the island has received almost no fuel in 2026. On May 13, Cuba’s energy minister said the country has completely run out of the diesel and fuel oil it needs to keep its power plants running.

title: Germany to Boost Raw-Materials Fund to Reduce Reliance on China
title_cn: 德国将增加原材料基金以减少对中国的依赖
summary: Germany plans to increase the firepower of its national raw-materials fund by as much as half to €1.5 billion ($1.7 billion) as it seeks to reduce dependence on critical supplies from countries such as China and shore up supply chains. Officials are also considering expanding the vehicle into a sovereign wealth fund from 2028 onward.

title: Secretive Deals, Aggressive Demands Are New US Foreign Aid Tactics
title_cn: 秘密交易、激进要求是美国对外援助的新策略
summary: On a Friday last November, government officials representing Lesotho, a mountainous enclave in eastern South Africa, sat down in the capital Maseru for three hours of negotiations with Trump administration counterparts.

title: Ifo Sees 'Lower Growth,' But No Recession in Germany
title_cn: Ifo 认为德国“增长放缓”，但并未出现衰退
summary: Ifo President Clemens Fuest discusses Germany's business outlook as an expectations index by the institute rose to 83.8 in May from a revised 83.5 the previous month. "Businesses are trying to adapt to this new situation," he says. Speaking on Bloomberg Television, Fuest adds:  "We see lower growth in Germany, but we don't see a recession." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: NATO Welcomes Trump Troop Pledge as Allies Caught Off Guard
title_cn: 北约欢迎特朗普的增兵承诺，盟友措手不及
summary: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte welcomed an abrupt announcement by President Donald Trump to send an additional 5,000 troops to Poland even as the alliance’s top envoys braced for cuts in US military resources.

title: UK Leader Starmer Is a ‘Dud’ Dimbleby Says
title_cn: 丁布尔比称英国领导人斯塔默是个“哑巴”
summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is a "dud," veteran political journalist David Dimbleby tells Mishal Husain.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Kenyan President Rules Out Further Fuel-Tax Cuts After Protests
title_cn: 抗议后肯尼亚总统排除进一步削减燃油税的可能性
summary: Kenyan President William Ruto ruled out further cuts to fuel taxes, even after deadly protests over the high cost of diesel brought the nation’s capital to a standstill this week.

title: Chinese Missiles, Robots Find Warm Welcome in EU’s Backyard
title_cn: 中国导弹、机器人在欧盟后院受到热烈欢迎
summary: If the European Union has a red line in Serbia’s relationship to China, President Aleksandar Vucic may be getting close.

title: IMF Financial Counsellor on Global Economy, Cybersecurity
title_cn: 国际货币基金组织全球经济、网络安全财务顾问
summary: Tobias Adrian, the International Monetary Fund's Financial Counsellor and Monetary and Capital Markets Director, discusses the response by global economies to the supply shocks created by the Iran war as well as efforts to achieve a coordinated response to cyberattacks, as AI accelerates the threat. Adrian joined Lizzy Burden on Bloomberg Daybreak Europe. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UK-EU Summit Risks Delay Over Slow Progress and Weakened Starmer
title_cn: 英国-欧盟峰会因进展缓慢和斯塔默减弱而面临推迟的风险
summary: The next UK-European Union summit may be postponed as negotiations to deepen ties falter and Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s authority weakens.

title: Attack on UAE Nuclear Plant From Iraq ‘Warning Shot’ By Iran
title_cn: 伊朗向伊拉克“警告”袭击阿联酋核电站
summary: An attack on a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates has raised fears about the scope of Iran’s retaliation to a potential US resumption of strikes, with experts highlighting the greater role Tehran-backed militias in Iraq are playing in the war.

title: French Business Confidence Languishes Below Average
title_cn: 法国商业信心低于平均水平
summary: French business confidence remained weak in May, adding to evidence that fallout from the Iran war is weighing on demand in the euro area’s second-largest economy.

title: Probe Into Former Prince Andrew Assesses Potential Sex Crimes
title_cn: 对前安德鲁王子的调查评估了潜在的性犯罪
summary: British police investigating former prince Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor over potential misconduct in public office are also assessing the possibility of sex crimes dating back to 2010.

title: UK Posts Highest April Budget Deficit Since the Pandemic
title_cn: 英国公布大流行以来最高的四月份预算赤字
summary: UK government borrowing hit the highest level for any April in six years, as pressure on the public finances mounts from the Iran war and political instability at home.

title: EU’s Dombrovskis Sees ECB Rate Response to Inflation Shock
title_cn: 欧盟多布罗夫斯基斯预计欧洲央行将采取利率应对通胀冲击的举措
summary: European Union economy chief Valdis Dombrovskis said the European Central Bank will need to address the continent’s rising inflation due to the war in Iran.

title: US Sanctions Tanzanian Police Chief Over Alleged Rights Abuses
title_cn: 美国因涉嫌侵犯人权而制裁坦桑尼亚警察局长
summary: The US sanctioned a Tanzanian police chief over the alleged torture of detained human-rights activists in the lead-up to last year’s disputed presidential elections.

title: Turkish Court Unseats Leader of Main Opposition Party
title_cn: 土耳其法院罢免主要反对党领袖
summary: A Turkish court removed the leader of the country’s main opposition party, the Republican People’s Party (CHP), in a landmark ruling that led to a stock market selloff and state intervention to defend the lira. The court decision voids the election of Ozgur Ozel as CHP chairman. Bloomberg's Beril Akman reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Irish Elections to Showcase Voter Anger After Fuel Protests
title_cn: 爱尔兰选举将在燃料抗议后展现选民的愤怒
summary: Ireland is about to get another lesson in how quickly its political landscape is shifting in two elections on Friday on opposite sides of the country.

title: Iran Talks Progress Undercut by Hormuz, Uranium Divide
title_cn: 伊朗谈判因霍尔木兹海峡和铀鸿沟而削弱进展
summary: US President Donald Trump said he opposed efforts by Iran and Oman to establish some form of permanent toll system through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran said the latest US proposal partly bridged the gap between the warring sides, but comments from the Islamic Republic’s supreme leader about keeping Tehran’s uranium stockpile and a dispute over tolls in the key waterway clouded the outlook for a breakthrough. Bloomberg's Stuart Livingstone-Wallace reports. (Source: Bloomberg)
